summary: The PR’s reminder-delivery hardening is directionally correct, but the new validation contract is incomplete: it requires a `status` field without constraining it to `sent|missed`, even though `complete-reminder` rejects any other value. That leaves a malformed handoff able to pass ‘validation’ and fail during completion, stranding delivery. No workflow-permission or reviewer-routing files changed.

blocking_issues.message: The new reminder-handoff validation contract only requires each entry to have a `status` field, but `scripts/notion-cli.sh complete-reminder` accepts only `sent` or `missed` (`scripts/notion-cli.sh:165-176`). As written, a handoff with `status: "foo"` still satisfies the documented validation step in AGENTS.md, HEARTBEAT.md, and setup/cron/reminder-check.md, then fails during completion instead of being rejected up front. Tighten the spec so any status outside `sent|missed` makes the handoff malformed and prevents send/complete/delete behavior.

non_blocking_notes.message: The PR updates the runtime-facing reminder-delivery contract in AGENTS.md, HEARTBEAT.md, and setup docs, but docs/architecture.md still describes the older flow without the new explicit handoff validation and `message`-tool delivery language. Consider reconciling the background architecture docs in a follow-up to keep operator guidance aligned.

fix_suggestions.patch_hint: In AGENTS.md:11, HEARTBEAT.md:11, and setup/cron/reminder-check.md:25, change the validation rule from ‘each entry has `page_id`, `title`, and `status`’ to ‘each entry has `page_id`, `title`, and `status`, where `status` is exactly `sent` or `missed`’. Add one sentence that any other status value makes the handoff malformed, so the agent must leave the file in place and must not call `complete-reminder` or delete the handoff file.
